frontMatter.title: Building the Ultimate DIY NAS: ZimaBoard 2 Review & Benchmarks
frontMatter.author: Amrut Prabhu
frontMatter.summary: Build the ultimate compact DIY NAS with ZimaBoard 2. Leveraging ZimaOS and dual 2.5GbE, this energy-efficient kit simplifies backups, media streaming, and running virtual machines for your personal home lab

CPU.Cores:
4 × Intel N150 Cores
Frequency up to 3.60GHz
6M Cache

specifications.AI Capabilities:
CPU Only.
Expandable via PCIe (e.g., external GPU support)
specifications.Storage:
Onboard 32GB / 64GB eMMC
2 × SATA 3.0 6Gb/s Ports (with power)
Expandable via PCIe 3.0 slot
specifications.Operating System:
[ZimaOS](https://www.zimaspace.com/zimaos) (Pre-installed)
Supports CasaOS, Debian, Ubuntu, Windows, OpenWrt, pfSense

prev.author: Amrut Prabhu
prev.summary: Powerful Radxa Orion O6N Nano-ITX review: benchmarks, Linux support, thermals, AI tests, and I/O performance with UEFI support.

suggestedArticles.title: Radxa Cubie A7A Review: Pi-Sized Power With PCIe, NVMe, USB 3.1 Gen2
Radxa Cubie A5E Hands-On: The Budget NVMe SBC
I Built A DIY 10 Inch Server Rack

CPU.Cores:
4 × Cortex-A720 up to 2.6GHz
4 × Cortex-A720 up to 2.4GHz
4 × Cortex-A520 up to 1.8GHz

GPU.Support:
Vulkan 1.4
OpenGL ES 4.3
OpenCL 3.0
Hardware ray tracing

specifications.Video Output:
1 × DisplayPort 1.4 (up to 4K@120Hz, MST supported)
1 × HDMI 2.0 (up to 4K@60Hz)
1 × USB-C (USB 3.2 Gen2 + DisplayPort Alt Mode, up to 4K@60Hz)
specifications.USB:
1 × USB-C (USB 3.2 Gen2 10Gbps + DP Alt Mode)
2 × USB-A (USB 3.2 Gen2 10Gbps)
3 × USB-A (USB 2.0)
specifications.Power:
12V DC via 5525 Barrel Jack
12V DC via 4-pin Power Connector.
specifications.Audio: No dedicated 3.5mm audio jack (audio via HDMI/DP)
specifications.Cooling:
4-pin CPU fan header with PWM control + TACH feedback
75×75mm heatsink mounting holes
specifications.Other Expansion Capabilities:
1 × M.2 E-Key (PCIe Gen4 x2 + USB) for wireless modules
1 × M.2 B-Key (USB) for cellular modules
1 x Nano SIM slot
40-pin GPIO header
